caption: Figure 1: Schematic of a cMDP with common state & action spaces with transition, reward, and initial-state distributions conditioned on a context 𝑐. The diagram shows two learning pipelines: (i) context-oblivious policy 𝜋(𝑠) receives only the state observation; (ii) context-aware policy 𝜛(𝑠,𝑐) additionally observes the context. Both are trained across contexts 𝑐 ∼ C.
description2: The figure depicts a cMDP with common state and action spaces, featuring transition, reward, and initial-state distributions conditioned on a context, showing two learning pipelines: one for a context-oblivious policy and another for a context-aware policy.
description3: A block diagram displays a policy and environment interaction, with context conditioning one policy and state observations for the other.
questions: How does the context influence the initial-state distributions in this learning setup?
What are the key differences in how observations are used by each learning pipeline?
